name: Preliminary Phase
description: Define Architecture Capability, establish Architecture Principles, select tools, define frameworks and methods

inputs: Board strategies and board business plans

name: Phase A: Architecture Vision
description: Develop high-level aspirational vision, obtain approval for Statement of Architecture Work

outputs: Refined statements of business principles, goals, and drivers
steps: Identify stakeholders, concerns, and business requirements
Confirm and elaborate business goals, drivers, and constraints
Define Target Architecture value propositions and KPIs

name: Phase B: Business Architecture
description: Develop the Business Architecture that supports the Architecture Vision

outputs: Draft Architecture Definition Document (updated with Business Architecture)
Business Architecture components of Architecture Roadmap
steps: Select reference models, viewpoints, and tools
Resolve impacts across the Architecture Landscape

name: Phase C: Information Systems Architectures
description: Develop Data Architecture and Application Architecture to support Business Architecture

outputs: Draft Architecture Definition Document (updated with Data and Application Architectures)
Data Architecture components of Architecture Roadmap
Application Architecture components of Architecture Roadmap
steps: Develop Data Architecture (select models/viewpoints, baseline, target, gap analysis)
Develop Application Architecture (select models/viewpoints, baseline, target, gap analysis)
Resolve impacts across the Architecture Landscape

name: Phase D: Technology Architecture
description: Develop the Technology Architecture that enables the IS Architectures

outputs: Draft Architecture Definition Document (updated with Technology Architecture)
Technology Architecture components of Architecture Roadmap
steps: Select reference models, viewpoints, and tools
Resolve impacts across the Architecture Landscape

name: Phase E: Opportunities and Solutions
description: Generate the initial complete version of the Architecture Roadmap, based on gap analysis and candidate roadmap components from B-D

inputs: Candidate Architecture Roadmap components from B-D
steps: Review and consolidate gap analysis results from B-D
Confirm readiness and risk for transformation
Identify and group major work packages
Create the Architecture Roadmap and Implementation and Migration Plan

name: Phase F: Migration Planning
description: Finalize the Architecture Roadmap and the Implementation and Migration Plan

inputs: Implementation Factor Assessment and Deduction Matrix
steps: Confirm management framework interactions for Implementation and Migration Plan
Assign a business value to each work package
Estimate resource requirements, project timings, and availability
Prioritize the migration projects through a cost/benefit assessment and risk validation
Confirm Architecture Roadmap and update Architecture Definition Document
Complete the Implementation and Migration Plan
Complete the architecture development cycle and document lessons learned

name: Phase G: Implementation Governance
description: Provide architecture oversight of the implementation, ensure conformance with Target Architecture

steps: Confirm scope and priorities for deployment with development management
Perform post-implementation review and close the implementation

name: Phase H: Architecture Change Management
description: Establish and support the implemented architecture, manage changes in an orderly fashion

outputs: Changes to Architecture Framework and Principles
New Request for Architecture Work (to initiate another ADM cycle)
steps: Provide analysis for architecture change management
Develop change requirements to meet performance targets
Activate the process to implement change

name: Requirements Management
description: Continuous process of managing architecture requirements throughout all ADM phases

inputs: Architecture requirements from any ADM phase
steps: Identify changed requirements and record priorities
Assess impact of changed requirements on current and previous ADM phases
Implement requirements arising from Phase H
Assess and revise gap analysis for changed requirements

name: Architecture Building Blocks
description: Reusable architecture components: ABBs capture architecture requirements and direct SBBs

name: Architecture Contract
description: Joint agreements between development partners and sponsors on deliverables, quality, and fitness-for-purpose of an architecture

name: Architecture Definition Document
description: The deliverable container for the core architectural artifacts. Spans all domains: Business, Data, Application, Technology

name: Architecture Principles
description: General rules and guidelines that inform and support how an organization fulfills its mission

name: Architecture Repository
description: The holding area for all architecture-related materials: Architecture Metamodel, Architecture Capability, Architecture Landscape, Standards Information Base, Reference Library, Governance Log

name: Architecture Requirements Specification
description: Set of quantitative statements that outline what an implementation project must do to comply with the architecture

name: Architecture Roadmap
description: List of individual work packages showing a timeline of the changes, including Transition Architectures

name: Architecture Vision
description: High-level summary of the changes to the enterprise that will accrue from successful deployment of the Target Architecture

name: Business Principles, Business Goals, and Business Drivers
description: Business principles, goals, and strategic drivers that provide context for architecture work

name: Capability Assessment
description: Assessment of the capabilities and maturity levels of the enterprise, identifying areas of change

name: Change Request
description: Request for modification, raised through governance as a result of changes to the baseline or target architecture

name: Communications Plan
description: Plan to communicate architecture deliverables to the right stakeholders at the right time

name: Compliance Assessment
description: Assessment of architecture compliance of projects, identifying any architecture gaps

name: Implementation Governance Model
description: Governance procedures and organization for managing the architecture implementation

name: Implementation and Migration Plan
description: Schedule of projects, resource allocations, and detailed migration approach for realizing Transition Architectures

name: Organizational Model for Enterprise Architecture
description: Governance model, maturity assessments, budget, and roles/responsibilities for EA capability

name: Request for Architecture Work
description: Trigger document for architecture development, from sponsor to architecture organization

name: Requirements Impact Assessment
description: Assessment of the impact of new/changed requirements on the current architecture

name: Solution Building Blocks
description: Candidate solutions: implementations of ABBs that map to specific products or technologies

name: Statement of Architecture Work
description: Defines scope and approach of the architecture project; a deliverable of Phase A

name: Tailored Architecture Framework
description: Organization-specific version of the TOGAF framework

name: Transition Architecture
description: Description of the enterprise at an architecturally significant state between the Baseline and Target Architectures

name: Principles Catalog
description: Catalog of architecture principles with rationale and implications

name: Stakeholder Map
description: Matrix of stakeholders against architecture viewpoints

name: Role Catalog
description: Catalog of governance/process roles

name: Business Service/Function Catalog
description: Functional decomposition of services/functions

name: Location Catalog
description: List of all enterprise locations

name: Process/Event/Control/Product Catalog
description: List of processes, events, controls, and products

name: Contract/Measure Catalog
description: Catalog of contracts and SLAs

name: Data Entity/Data Component Catalog
description: Listing of data entities and data components

name: Application Portfolio Catalog
description: Full inventory of applications

name: Interface Catalog
description: Catalog of application-to-application interfaces

name: Technology Standards Catalog
description: Approved technology standards list

name: Technology Portfolio Catalog
description: Technology components with lifecycle status

name: Stakeholder Map/Role Matrix
description: Mapping of stakeholders to their roles

name: Business Interaction Matrix
description: Dependencies between business functions/organizations

name: Actor/Role Matrix
description: Mapping of actors to roles

name: Application/Data Matrix
description: Mapping of applications to data entities

name: Application/Function Matrix
description: Mapping of applications to business functions

name: Application/Organization Matrix
description: Mapping of applications to organizational units

name: Application/Role Matrix
description: Mapping of applications to user roles

name: Application/Technology Matrix
description: Mapping of applications to technology platforms

name: System/Data Matrix
description: Mapping of systems to data flows

name: System/Technology Matrix
description: Mapping of systems to technology components

name: Value Chain Diagram
description: High-level view of the enterprise value chain

name: Solution Concept Diagram
description: High-level graphical view of the solution

name: Business Footprint Diagram
description: Links between business goals, organizational units, business functions, and services

name: Business Service/Information Diagram
description: Mapping of business services to information

name: Functional Decomposition Diagram
description: Hierarchical decomposition of business functions

name: Product Lifecycle Diagram
description: Lifecycle stages of products/services

name: Goal/Objective/Service Diagram
description: Mapping of business goals to architecture services

name: Business Use-Case Diagram
description: Business processes with actors

name: Organization Decomposition Diagram
description: Organizational hierarchy

name: Process Flow Diagram
description: Business process flow sequences

name: Event Diagram
description: Business events and their relationships

name: Data Dissemination Diagram
description: Data movement between systems

name: Data Lifecycle Diagram
description: Data entity lifecycle

name: Data Security Diagram
description: Data classification and access controls

name: Class Hierarchy Diagram
description: Data entity class hierarchy

name: Data Migration Diagram
description: Data migration approach

name: Application Communication Diagram
description: Inter-application data flows

name: Application and User Location Diagram
description: Geographic deployment of applications

name: Application Use-Case Diagram
description: Application use cases

name: Enterprise Manageability Diagram
description: How systems are managed

name: Process/Application Realization Diagram
description: How applications realize business processes

name: Software Engineering Diagram
description: Software engineering artifacts

name: Application Migration Diagram
description: Application migration steps

name: Software Distribution Diagram
description: Software deployment topology

name: Environments and Locations Diagram
description: Technology environments and physical locations

name: Platform Decomposition Diagram
description: Platform component breakdown

name: Processing Diagram
description: Processing flows and nodes

name: Networked Computing/Hardware Diagram
description: Network and compute infrastructure

name: Communications Engineering Diagram
description: Communication infrastructure detail

name: Principle
description: A qualitative statement of intent that should be met by the architecture

name: Constraint
description: An external factor that prevents the organization from pursuing approaches

name: Assumption
description: A statement of probable fact that has not been fully validated

name: Requirement
description: A quantitative statement of business need that must be met by architecture

name: Gap
description: A statement of difference between two states. Used in gap analysis for each domain

name: Work Package
description: A set of actions identified to realize one or more objectives of the architecture

name: Capability
description: A particular ability that a business may possess or exchange to achieve a purpose

name: Course of Action
description: Direction and focus for how the architecture contributes to achieving goals

name: Location
description: A place where business activity takes place and can be hierarchically decomposed

name: Organization Unit
description: Self-contained unit of resources with goals, objectives, and measures

name: Actor
description: A person, organization, or system that is outside the consideration of the architecture model

name: Role
description: An actor assumes a role to perform a task. Multiple actors can assume same role

name: Business Service
description: Supports business capabilities through an explicitly defined interface

name: Function
description: Delivers business capabilities closely aligned to an organization

name: Process
description: A process that is a flow of activities. Orchestrated through business events

name: Event
description: An organizational state change that triggers further processing

name: Control
description: Ensures that a process produces the correct outcome

name: Product
description: Output generated by the business that provides value to a customer

name: Measure
description: Indicator or factor that can be tracked, usually against a target or objective

name: Objective
description: A time-bounded milestone for an organization

name: Contract
description: An agreement between a service consumer and provider

name: Service Quality
description: A preset/agreed-to QoS level: availability, service hours, etc.

name: Data Entity
description: An encapsulation of data managed/owned by the enterprise

name: Logical Data Component
description: A boundary zone that encapsulates related data entities

name: Physical Data Component
description: A physical implementation of a logical data component

name: Logical Application Component
description: An encapsulation of application functionality independently deployable, reusable

name: Physical Application Component
description: An application, module, sub-system, or object that realizes logical components

name: Information System Service
description: An automated service that provides a specific business interface

name: Platform Service
description: A technical capability required to provide infrastructure for the architecture

name: Logical Technology Component
description: A technology infrastructure component independent of specific products

name: Physical Technology Component
description: A specific technology infrastructure product/component

name: Architecture Principles
description: Defining and cataloging architecture principles with name, statement, rationale, and implications

name: Stakeholder Management
description: Identifying, classifying, and managing stakeholder engagement using power/interest grids

name: Architecture Patterns
description: Reusable forms describing context, problem, and solution for recurring architectural challenges

name: Gap Analysis
description: Systematic comparison of baseline and target architectures to identify gaps (new, modified, eliminated, retained)

name: Migration Planning Techniques
description: Implementation Factor Assessment, Deduction Matrix, business value assessment, and risk assessment for sequencing

name: Interoperability Requirements
description: Determining interoperability requirements and classifying them on the Enterprise/ISA Interoperability spectrum

name: Business Transformation Readiness Assessment
description: Evaluating organizational readiness for change across maturity factors and readiness factors

name: Risk Management
description: Classification and mitigation of risks: initial level of risk, residual level of risk, and risk mitigation activities

name: Capability-Based Planning
description: Planning based on business capabilities: capability increments, capability dimensions, and heat maps

name: Business Scenarios
description: Technique for identifying and articulating business requirements using problem-goal-solution narrative

name: Value Realization/Business Value Assessment
description: Quantifying the business value of architecture work: financial metrics, KPIs, and value streams

name: Architecture Compliance Reviews
description: Formal reviews ensuring implementation conforms to architecture: tailored checklists per project type

name: Architecture Maturity Models
description: ACMM (Architecture Capability Maturity Model) levels 0-5 for assessing EA practice maturity

name: Implementation Factor Assessment and Deduction Matrix
description: Assessing factors (risks, issues, dependencies, actions) and using deduction matrix to determine project sequencing

name: Business Architecture View
description: Addresses concerns of business management: services, processes, organization, functions, events

name: Data Architecture View
description: Addresses concerns of data management: entities, components, flows, governance

name: Application Architecture View
description: Addresses concerns of application development: components, interfaces, services, portfolios

name: Technology Architecture View
description: Addresses concerns of IT operations: platforms, infrastructure, networks, middleware

name: Security Architecture View
description: Addresses security aspects across all architecture domains

name: Performance and Capacity Architecture View
description: Addresses performance and capacity engineering across all domains

name: System/Service Interoperability View
description: Addresses interoperability concerns between systems and services

name: Governance Architecture View
description: Addresses concerns of architecture governance: compliance, decision-making, standards

name: Migration Architecture View
description: Addresses concerns of transition from baseline to target architecture

name: Requirements View
description: Addresses traceability from requirements to architecture artifacts

name: Resource
description: An asset owned or controlled by an individual or organization

name: Capability
description: An ability that an active structure element possesses

name: Value Stream
description: A sequence of activities that creates an overall result for a customer, stakeholder, or end user

name: Course of Action
description: An approach or plan for configuring capabilities and resources to achieve a goal

name: Business Actor
description: A business entity that is capable of performing behavior

name: Business Role
description: The responsibility for performing specific behavior, to which an actor can be assigned

name: Business Collaboration
description: An aggregate of two or more business internal active structure elements that work together

name: Business Interface
description: A point of access where a business service is made available to the environment

name: Business Process
description: A sequence of business behaviors that achieves a specific result

name: Business Function
description: A collection of business behavior based on a chosen set of criteria

name: Business Interaction
description: A unit of collective business behavior performed by a collaboration

name: Business Event
description: A business behavior element that denotes an organizational state change

name: Business Service
description: An explicitly defined exposed business behavior

name: Business Object
description: A concept used within a particular business domain

name: Contract
description: A formal or informal specification of an agreement between a provider and a consumer

name: Representation
description: A perceptible form of the information carried by a business object

name: Product
description: A coherent collection of services and/or passive structure elements, accompanied by a contract/agreement

name: Application Component
description: An encapsulation of application functionality aligned to implementation structure

name: Application Collaboration
description: An aggregate of two or more application components that work together

name: Application Interface
description: A point of access where application services are made available

name: Application Function
description: Automated behavior that can be performed by an application component

name: Application Interaction
description: A unit of collective application behavior performed by a collaboration

name: Application Process
description: A sequence of application behaviors that achieves a specific result

name: Application Event
description: An application behavior element that denotes a state change

name: Application Service
description: An explicitly defined exposed application behavior

name: Data Object
description: Data structured for automated processing

name: Node
description: A computational or physical resource that hosts, manipulates, or interacts with other computational or physical resources

name: Device
description: A physical IT resource upon which system software and artifacts may be stored or deployed

name: System Software
description: Software that provides or contributes to an environment for storing, executing, and using software or data

name: Technology Collaboration
description: An aggregate of two or more technology internal active structure elements that work together

name: Technology Interface
description: A point of access where technology services are made available

name: Path
description: A link between two or more nodes, through which these nodes can exchange data, energy, or material

name: Communication Network
description: A set of structures that connects nodes for transmission, routing, and reception

name: Technology Function
description: A collection of technology behavior that can be performed by a node

name: Technology Process
description: A sequence of technology behaviors that achieves a specific result

name: Technology Interaction
description: A unit of collective technology behavior performed by a collaboration

name: Technology Event
description: A technology behavior element that denotes a state change

name: Technology Service
description: An explicitly defined exposed technology behavior

name: Artifact
description: A piece of data that is used or produced in a software development process, or by deployment and operation of an IT system

name: Equipment
description: One or more physical machines, tools, or instruments that can create, use, store, move, or transform materials

name: Facility
description: A physical structure or environment

name: Distribution Network
description: A physical medium used to transport materials or energy

name: Material
description: Tangible physical matter or energy

name: Stakeholder
description: The role of an individual, team, or organization that represents their interests in the architecture

name: Driver
description: An external or internal condition that motivates an organization to define its goals and implement changes

name: Assessment
description: The result of an analysis of the state of affairs of the enterprise with respect to some driver

name: Goal
description: A high-level statement of intent, direction, or desired end state

name: Outcome
description: An end result

name: Principle
description: A qualitative statement of intent that should be met by the architecture

name: Requirement
description: A statement of need that must be realized by a system

name: Constraint
description: A factor that prevents or obstructs the realization of goals

name: Meaning
description: The knowledge or expertise present in, or the interpretation given to, a concept

name: Value
description: The relative worth, utility, or importance of a concept

name: Work Package
description: A series of actions identified and designed to achieve specific results within specified time and resource constraints

name: Deliverable
description: A precisely-defined outcome of a work package

name: Implementation Event
description: A state change related to implementation or migration

name: Plateau
description: A relatively stable state of the architecture that exists during a limited period of time

name: Gap
description: A statement of difference between two plateaus

name: Grouping
description: Aggregates or composes concepts that belong together based on some common characteristic

name: Location
description: A place or position where structure elements can be located or behavior can be performed

name: Composition Relationship
description: Indicates that an element consists of one or more other concepts. The part is integral to the whole

name: Aggregation Relationship
description: Indicates that an element combines one or more other concepts. Parts can exist independently

name: Assignment Relationship
description: Expresses the allocation of responsibility, performance of behavior, storage, or execution

name: Realization Relationship
description: Indicates that an entity plays a critical role in the creation, achievement, sustenance, or operation of a more abstract entity

name: Serving Relationship
description: Models that an element provides its functionality to another element. Previously called 'Used By'

name: Access Relationship
description: Models the ability of behavior and active structure elements to observe or act upon passive structure elements

name: Influence Relationship
description: Models that an element affects the implementation or achievement of some motivation element. Can be positive or negative

name: Triggering Relationship
description: Describes a temporal or causal relationship between elements. The source triggers the target

name: Flow Relationship
description: Describes the exchange or transfer of, for example, information or value between processes, function, interactions, and events

name: Specialization Relationship
description: Indicates that an element is a particular kind of another element

name: Association Relationship
description: Models an unspecified relationship, or one that is not represented by another ArchiMate relationship

name: Junction (AND)
description: Used to connect relationships of the same type. AND junction: all paths are followed

name: Junction (OR)
description: Used to connect relationships of the same type. OR junction: one or more paths are followed

name: Organization Viewpoint
description: Shows the structure of the organization: actors, roles, and their relationships

name: Business Process Cooperation Viewpoint
description: Shows relationships and dependencies between business processes

name: Product Viewpoint
description: Shows the value a product offers to customers, including the constituting services and contracts

name: Application Cooperation Viewpoint
description: Shows application components and their cooperation through information flows or services

name: Application Usage Viewpoint
description: Relates applications to their use by business processes

name: Implementation and Deployment Viewpoint
description: Shows how applications are mapped onto the underlying technology

name: Technology Viewpoint
description: Shows the structure and connectivity of the technology infrastructure

name: Technology Usage Viewpoint
description: Shows how applications are supported by the technology infrastructure

name: Information Structure Viewpoint
description: Shows the structure of the information used, including data types and relationships

name: Service Realization Viewpoint
description: Shows how services are realized by required behavior, performed by active structure elements

name: Physical Viewpoint
description: Shows the physical environment and how it relates to IT infrastructure

name: Layered Viewpoint
description: Provides an overview across two or more layers of the ArchiMate framework

name: Landscape Map Viewpoint
description: Represents architecture elements as a map (matrix/grid) showing relationships between dimensions

name: Goal Realization Viewpoint
description: Shows how goals are refined into requirements, and realized by core elements

name: Requirements Realization Viewpoint
description: Shows how requirements are realized by core elements such as actors, services, processes, applications

name: Motivation Viewpoint
description: Shows motivational elements and their relationships: stakeholders, drivers, goals, principles, requirements

name: Strategy Viewpoint
description: Shows strategic course of action, capabilities, and resources

name: Capability Map Viewpoint
description: Shows a structured overview of capabilities, typically as a heat map

name: Outcome Realization Viewpoint
description: Shows how outcomes contribute to value creation and how core elements realize outcomes

name: Resource Map Viewpoint
description: Shows structured overview of resources required to realize capabilities

name: Value Stream Viewpoint
description: Shows value stream stages and their relationships to capabilities, value, and stakeholders

name: Project Viewpoint
description: Shows management of the change: work packages, deliverables, and migration from plateau to plateau

name: Migration Viewpoint
description: Shows transition from existing plateaus to target plateaus through transition architectures

name: Implementation and Migration Viewpoint
description: Shows how the implementation of projects relates to migration from current to future state
